raw_consumer_statement:
This is a follow-up to a previous CFPB complaint ( Reference # XXXX ) regarding a disputed charge. My credit card company responded, but isnt responding directly to my dispute. They need to provide additional evidence proving receipt of goods. 

In the original dispute, I explained that a substantial portion of the charge was for XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  which were never provided to me during my visit. I submitted a sworn statement confirming that I never received or used the credits, which constitutes a failure to deliver services a valid basis for a dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ).

Despite this, the credit card company recycled their original denial and failed to address the core issue : the non-receipt of services. Their response ignored my sworn statement and made no indication that any further investigation was performed after new evidence was submitted. They simply are showing receipts that I signed for the purchase, but the agreement is signed for was implying the amount of cash I paid equated to these gift certificates.

This is a violation of their duty to conduct a good faith investigation under the FCBA. A signed receipt or agreement does not excuse a merchant from delivering the services paid for.

I am requesting that the credit card company be required to re-investigate the dispute based on the actual claim ( non-receipt of goods/services ), and that the portion of the charge related to the undelivered XXXX XXXX credits be refunded. 

Supporting documentation, including the sworn statement, can be provided again if necessary.
